gui.dungeontrain.book_vote.prompt.6: "Shall the library car keep this one?"
gui.dungeontrain.book_vote.prompt.8: "Was this a good use of a quiet mile?"
The train takes note. This is a good book.
Noted. The rails hum a little brighter.
The conductor stamps it: worth the read.
This one earns a window seat.
Your praise is shoveled into the firebox. Onward.
The library car shelves it with pride.
The train will whisper this title to other travellers.
A fine read. More carriages will carry it.
The train remembers the good ones.
gui.dungeontrain.book_vote.response.no.1: The train takes note. This one may miss its stop.
gui.dungeontrain.book_vote.response.no.2: Noted. The library car quietly closes its cover.
gui.dungeontrain.book_vote.response.no.3: The conductor frowns and files it away.
gui.dungeontrain.book_vote.response.no.4: Not every page earns its passage.
gui.dungeontrain.book_vote.response.no.5: This one gets a seat in the caboose.
gui.dungeontrain.book_vote.response.no.6: The wheels grind a low apology to the author.
gui.dungeontrain.book_vote.response.no.7: Fewer carriages will carry this one.
gui.dungeontrain.book_vote.response.no.8: The train hears you. Some stories ride quieter now.
gui.dungeontrain.book_vote.response.gene: Your verdict is logged in the ledger car.
The conductor nods and moves on.
Noted, between one station and the next.
The library car updates its records.
The rails carry your verdict down the line.
The train files it away with a quiet whistle.
So noted. The wheels keep turning.
Every reader steers the shelves a little.
chat.dungeontrain.free_play.title: Free Play.
chat.dungeontrain.free_play.cause.ais_da: The Adventure Item Stats settings have been changed.
chat.dungeontrain.free_play.consequence: Advancements you earn here won't be saved to your profile, stats won't count toward your global totals, and your Ender Chest swaps to a separate Creative one so your saved items stay safe.
chat.dungeontrain.free_play.ais_fix: Click here to restore the default settings, then restart the game to play normally.
command.dungeontrain.fix_ais.success: Default Adventure Item Stats settings restored. Restart the game (or server) to resume normal play.
command.dungeontrain.fix_ais.already_def: Adventure Item Stats settings were already at their defaults — restart the game if you still see Free Play.
command.dungeontrain.fix_ais.fail: Couldn't write the Adventure Item Stats settings file — see the game log.
effect.dungeontrain.free_play.desc.1: Advancements earned here won't be saved to your profile.
effect.dungeontrain.free_play.desc.2: Stats won't count toward your global totals.
effect.dungeontrain.free_play.desc.3: Your Ender Chest switches to your Creative one — your saved items stay safe.
gui.dungeontrain.free_play.confirm.title: Enter Free Play?
gui.dungeontrain.free_play.confirm.body: Free Play stays on for the rest of this run. Nothing is lost — but from here on:
gui.dungeontrain.consent.feature.book_sh: Share books you write for others to find
gui.dungeontrain.consent.feature.reincar: Reincarnate as a mob in other players' worlds
gui.dungeontrain.loading.title: Loading the world…
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.1.line.2: The train is a curious thing.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.1.line.4: A carriage adrift in liminal space and time.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.1.line.5: Others, a dungeon crawler with a view.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.1.line.7: A madman once claimed it a physics mod.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.1.line.9: Dreamed up by an award-winning hand.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.1.line.10: Does the answer change the ride?
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.1.line.11: You'll have time to decide. The ride is long.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.2.line.1: What does the train want from me?
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.2.line.4: infinite, and never the same twice.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.3.line.4: They wear the faces of people —
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.3.line.8: Some never know you were there at all.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.3.line.9: You will not meet the same one twice.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.3.line.11: The old riders speak of echoes.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.3.line.12: Echoes of those who passed before.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.3.line.16: I try not to look too closely.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.4.line.6: Many simply green fields. Lush forests.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.4.line.8: Fewer speak of fire. Of voids without end.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.4.line.12: Fewer still speak of the Creator —
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.4.line.15: They say his passion will not let him rest.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.4.line.24: where the living and the Creator
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.4.line.28: every idea, every grievance, every prayer —
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.4.line.29: and folds it back into the train.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.4.line.30: If you have a word to give,
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.4.line.33: I stopped believing in the destination.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.5.line.2: They say a man made it.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.5.line.6: It's said he has a human name
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.5.line.11: They say he built the first car
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.5.line.12: out of something he couldn't put down —
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.5.line.16: long after everyone else stopped looking.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.5.line.17: Some say he's still building it.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.5.line.19: was laid down after you boarded,
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.5.line.21: That the track ahead of you
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.5.line.23: I don't know if that's true.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.5.line.25: the parts of the train I like best
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.5.line.26: feel like someone was still deciding
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.5.line.29: Maybe that's what a creator is.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.6.line.1: Where did the train come from?
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.6.line.2: Some claim it was always here,
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.6.line.4: before there were worlds to loop through.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.6.line.5: Others say a door opened once —
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.6.line.7: and the train was what came through it,
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.6.line.9: The old maps show no station.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.6.line.10: No first platform, no departure gate,
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.6.line.11: no whistle to mark the start.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.6.line.14: A few swear it was built.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.6.line.15: By hands, by hunger, by grief —
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.6.line.16: that the Creator laid the first car down
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.6.line.17: the way you'd lay down a wish,
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.6.line.18: not knowing it would run this long,
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.6.line.21: Me, I think it came from wanting.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.7.line.1: What does the train dream about?
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.7.line.3: Then I found a carriage once
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.7.line.5: furniture from a decade before mine,
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.7.line.6: a smell like a house I grew up in,
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.7.line.7: gone before I could tell anyone.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.7.line.8: The old riders call these "loose cars."
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.7.line.10: the way you have a thought
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.7.line.13: it dreams of every rider it's had.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.7.line.16: the way a hundred voices in a room
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.7.line.17: become a sound instead of words.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.7.line.18: Maybe it dreams of the fire.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.7.line.19: The ones who speak of it say
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.7.line.22: to reach the places without end.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.7.line.23: Or maybe it dreams the way the deep does.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.7.line.26: and the shape of something enormous
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.8.line.1: Does the train choose who boards?
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.8.line.2: Everyone wants it to be a choice.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.8.line.4: a gate that opens for the worthy,
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.8.line.6: But I've seen the cruel board.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.8.line.7: I've seen the kind get left
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.8.line.11: Some say the train doesn't choose at all.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.8.line.12: That the door opens for anyone
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.8.line.16: just not by the measure you'd use.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.8.line.21: I asked an old rider once,
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.8.line.22: did you choose this, or did it choose you?
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.8.line.25: By the time you're close enough to ask,
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.9.line.3: and you stop hearing about the Creator.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.9.line.9: left something behind without meaning to:
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.9.line.10: a name carved in a beam,
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.9.line.13: because they were in a hurry
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.9.line.14: and never came back to close it.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.9.line.17: They say new carriages don't get built —
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.9.line.19: Grown out of what riders drop behind them
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.9.line.22: decided not to take back out to sea.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.9.line.23: So maybe the Creator laid the first rail.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.9.line.27: every echo that knew your name
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.9.line.30: that got off the train already.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.9.line.31: Yours will be one of them.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.9.line.33: some rider who's never met you
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.9.line.34: is going to walk into a room
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.9.line.36: because you were once afraid in it,
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.9.line.38: or just quiet, for a while, in it.
gui.dungeontrain.loading.story.9.line.41: like someone was here before them,
gui.dungeontrain.death.narr.platform_fal: The platform waits, quiet. The line will carry you on whenever you are ready.
chat.dungeontrain.bed.no_respawn.1: Respawn point set — aboard the train. You'll wake here wherever the line has carried it.
chat.dungeontrain.bed.no_respawn.2: The rails carry your anchor now. Sleep, and the train decides where you wake.
chat.dungeontrain.bed.no_respawn.3: Rest set. The train will carry your respawn wherever it rolls.
chat.dungeontrain.bed.no_respawn.4: You'll wake aboard the train, wherever the line has taken it.
chat.dungeontrain.bed.no_respawn.5: The train is your only respawn point.
chat.dungeontrain.advancements_hint.1: All aboard! Press %s to see all your Dungeon Train advancements.
chat.dungeontrain.advancements_hint.2: All aboard! Your journey log awaits - press %s.
chat.dungeontrain.advancements_hint.3: Next stop: your achievements. Press %s to view all Dungeon Train advancements.
chat.dungeontrain.advancements_hint.4: The Conductor tips his hat - press %s to review your travels.
chat.dungeontrain.advancements_hint.5: The Conductor: "Press %s to check your ticket of accomplishments, passenger."
chat.dungeontrain.advancements_hint.6: The Conductor nods: press %s to see how far you've come.
chat.dungeontrain.advancements_hint.7: Punch your ticket of milestones - press %s.
chat.dungeontrain.advancements_hint.8: Another stop on the line! Press %s to see all Dungeon Train advancements.
chat.dungeontrain.advancements_hint.9: Mark it in your travel log - press %s to view every advancement.
chat.dungeontrain.advancements_hint.10: Toot toot! Press %s to see all your Dungeon Train advancements.
chat.dungeontrain.advancements_hint.11: The whistle blows - press %s to view every Dungeon Train advancement.
chat.dungeontrain.advancements_hint.12: Mind the gap - and press %s to see all your Dungeon Train advancements.
chat.dungeontrain.familiar_book.1: A familiar book. It feels like %1$s held it, and %2$s read it to the very end.
chat.dungeontrain.familiar_book.2: This book knows your hand — some %1$s held it; %2$s saw it through to the last page.
chat.dungeontrain.familiar_book.3: A familiar weight. Around %1$s held it, and one reader stayed with it for %2$s straight.
chat.dungeontrain.familiar_book.4: You've felt this before. %1$s held it; the longest reading ran %2$s.
chat.dungeontrain.familiar_book.5: A familiar book. %1$s held it, and someone lingered longest on page %2$s — %3$s there alone.
chat.dungeontrain.familiar_book.6: Your words come round again. %1$s held it; page %2$s held one reader for %3$s.
chat.dungeontrain.familiar_book.7: A familiar book. It feels like %1$s held it, opened %2$s in all.
chat.dungeontrain.familiar_book.8: This book remembers you. %1$s held it, its pages turned %2$s over.
chat.dungeontrain.familiar_book.9: A familiar book. %1$s held it — and %2$s someone couldn't help but read it again.
chat.dungeontrain.familiar_book.10: A familiar book. It feels like %1$s held your words.
chat.dungeontrain.deathnote.no_name: The Death Note finds no name; it burns to nothing.
chat.dungeontrain.deathnote.taken: The Death Note takes %s. When you fall, your echo will hunt them.
chat.dungeontrain.shared_book.1: The train has your book now. For someone else's eyes…
chat.dungeontrain.shared_book.2: Your words ride the rails now — bound for a stranger's hands.
chat.dungeontrain.shared_book.3: The book burns away. Somewhere down the line, someone will find it.
chat.dungeontrain.shared_book.4: Ash and ink. Your story travels on without you.
chat.dungeontrain.shared_book.5: The train swallows your book. It will surface in another traveler's chest.
chat.dungeontrain.shared_book.6: Gone to smoke — carried ahead to a reader you'll never meet.
chat.dungeontrain.shared_book.7: Your book is the train's now, to leave in a carriage far from here.
chat.dungeontrain.shared_book.8: The pages curl and vanish. They'll open again for someone else.
chat.dungeontrain.shared_book.9: Sealed, signed, surrendered. The rails will carry it to a stranger.
chat.dungeontrain.shared_book.10: Into the fire, and out into the world. May another find your words.
chat.dungeontrain.letter.sealed: "%1$s" is sealed and sent — Letter %2$s of this life.
dungeontrain.floor_y.label: Floor Y
dungeontrain.floor_y.tooltip: Bedrock floor elevation. Lower values preserve more of the vanilla deepslate/diamond zone; higher values improve performance. World top stays at Y=320.
gui.dungeontrain.menu_chat.title: Discord
gui.dungeontrain.menu_chat.empty: No messages yet. Say hi in Discord!
gui.dungeontrain.menu_chat.popup: The Dev sent you a message: "%s"
gui.dungeontrain.death.stats.title: Run Stats
gui.dungeontrain.death.narr.title: You died
gui.dungeontrain.death.gallery.title: photos from this run
gui.dungeontrain.death.gallery.subtitle: %s captured · click a photo to save it
gui.dungeontrain.death.gallery.empty: No photos were captured this run.
gui.dungeontrain.death.narr.bug_intro: The creator also keeps a fault-log — telling what broke helps mend the line.
gui.dungeontrain.death.narr.bug_comment_: Please share as much details as possible
gui.dungeontrain.death.narr.bug_log_noti: Your recent game logs will be attached to help diagnose this.
Your recent game logs plus basic system info (OS, allocated memory, CPU/GPU) will be attached to help diagnose lag.
gui.dungeontrain.death.narr.survey_intro: Before the train carries you back into the dark, the line's creator keeps a ledger — not of carriages, but of the travellers who ride.
The line's creator is begging now — this railway is slipping into the dark and cannot be kept alive alone. Whatever you say next, please, don't let it fade.
Please — the line is dying in the dark. One honest answer could be all that keeps the rails alight. Don't ride off in silence.
Don't leave it to the dark. This whole line was built on hope alone and it's faltering — whatever you say next could be what keeps the train moving.
Will you help before the dark takes the line for good? The creator can't keep these rails alight alone — one honest word might be all that's left to save them.
Could you spare one true word before you go? The line is failing in the dark, and a traveller's voice may be the only thing left that keeps it running.
Is there nothing you'd say to keep the line alive? It was built by hand, it's failing in the dark, and the travellers' words are all that hold the rails together now.
The line's creator pleads with every traveller who falls — would you speak? The dark is winning, the train is faltering, and these words are the only fuel the rails have left.
gui.dungeontrain.death.delete_world.on: This world will be deleted when you reboard
gui.dungeontrain.death.delete_world.off: This world will be kept when you reboard
gui.dungeontrain.death.discarding_world_: The rails remember, the world forgets…
The dungeon folds back into the dark…
The old track crumbles behind the train…
Nothing is left at the platform…
Returning the world to the void…
The Dungeon Train keeps no souvenirs…
gui.dungeontrain.support.title: Ways to Help
gui.dungeontrain.support.subtitle: Dungeon Train is made by one person. Here is how you can help!
gui.dungeontrain.support.financial.desc:
Support me on Patreon or with a one-off donation.
Or use my %s when you rent a multiplayer server.
gui.dungeontrain.support.donate_tooltip: No fees - custom amount - all goes direct to my bank account
gui.dungeontrain.support.share.desc:
Send it to content creators. Comment on their videos.
Make your own videos!
Share any Dungeon Train videos in Discord.
gui.dungeontrain.support.feedback.desc:
Share your feedback and ideas in Discord.
Test out a mod you want in the modpack, and tell me about it.
Tell me if you live stream Dungeon Train.
Join the %s to get involved.
gui.dungeontrain.editor_help.title: Dungeon Train Editor
gui.dungeontrain.editor_help.welcome_1: Welcome to the Dungeon Train Editor.
gui.dungeontrain.editor_help.welcome_2: Here you can add your own designs to
gui.dungeontrain.editor_help.welcome_3: the train, and send them to the developer.
gui.dungeontrain.editor_help.wiki_button: Read the Wiki to learn more
gui.dungeontrain.options.title: Dungeon Train — World Options
gui.dungeontrain.options.dims_unlock_war: This setting is deprecated and changing may break the game
gui.dungeontrain.options.range_hint: Train Y %s–%s  ·  L %s–%s  ·  W %s–%s  ·  H %s–%s  ·  Group %s–%s
gui.dungeontrain.settings.title: Dungeon Train Settings
gui.dungeontrain.settings.range_hint: Carriages %s-%s, Speed %s-%s, Train Y %s-%s, Group %s-%s, PlayerMob %s-%s
gui.dungeontrain.settings.hint_general: Train Y applies to next spawn only. Mode affects new carriage placements.
gui.dungeontrain.settings.hint_playermob: PlayerMob = 1-in-N ahead spawns; Behind = %% chance one also spawns a group behind you (THIS world).
PlayerMob = 1-in-N ahead spawns; Behind = %% chance one also spawns a group behind you (new worlds).
gui.dungeontrain.settings.hint_compat_te: Compat terrain = default for NEW worlds; lets terrain mods (Tectonic) + Distant Horizons generate.
gui.dungeontrain.settings.hint_note: Note: other settings require an active world; PlayerMob + Compat terrain defaults save here.
key.dungeontrain.variant_place: Place Block Variant (hold + right-click; tap to open menu)
key.dungeontrain.template_blocks: Open Template Blocks Menu (or /dungeontrain editor blocks)
gui.dungeontrain.developer_popup.title: A message from the developer
gui.dungeontrain.developer_popup.author: Brennan — Dev
gui.dungeontrain.developer_popup.message: Hey! What do you think of the game? I'd love to chat with you in Discord about what you think it needs :)
chat.dungeontrain.dev_message.consent_pr: The Developer wants to send you a message, type %s to accept.
chat.dungeontrain.presence.online_now: Brennan is online on Discord right now!
chat.dungeontrain.presence.last_seen: Brennan was last seen online %s ago.
Its not on the train.
Escaping the train?
Take the stairs deep underground.
Thats one way to get back board.
Why'd you leave in the first place?
Where do the lecterns go?
How many stories are there?
The stories change, re-reading is key.
The afterlife is just a feedback survey.
the biomes are counting.
the biomes are counting further.
thats a lot of biomes
There's still ground you haven't stood on.
The game is better with friends
You'll know it when you see it.
Be kind to yourself.
To become new, you must kill the old.
Not everyone needs to be onboard.
Do you have it in you?
Take it from them.
Give a little.
Get a little.
Lend a helping hand
You have many lifetimes to meet a lot of people.
Is that what the starting book said last time?
How many ways to start this are there?
Its in the books
They burnt regardless of how read they are
A whole inventory of stories. Why need anything else?
Try writing something down.
Some books only work once you're gone.
Some curses come full circle.
Someone wanted you gone.
Not every book in these chests was written by the game.
Then keep reading.
then read them
That one about opening chests x 100
How far through the train can you make it?
How far can you take that first book?
Make it really far.
Absurdly far.
Not every journey needs a fight.
Keep the peace a little longer.
How far can you go without lifting a finger?
Skip a carriage
Dont touch the ground, dont leave the train.
Lets log some hours. Dying is fine.
Dying resets it. How long can you stay?
Double it.
You'll likely need more then one life to hit this timeframe.
Investing crazy hours into the game
Please tell me you took breaks.
The final advancement.
Just stay aboard and dont die.
Keep riding.
Deaths don't matter, distance does.
Thats a long ride without dying.
Wow. In one life?
Sunken Cost Fallacy on going back?
Dungeon Train
All aboard.
Everything Burrito
Earn every other Dungeon Train advancement. The whole train, all wrapped up.
Track Record
Drop off the train and stand on the rails it rides. The ground moves differently down here.
Off the Rails
Leave the train and the tracks behind. The world is bigger than the corridor — and quieter.
Back on Track
Get off the train, then climb back aboard. From the rails or from a running leap — the train doesn't ask.
Taking the Stairs
Climb the stairs beside a bridge pillar. Somebody built these. Nobody knows who.
Beneath the Candy Kingdom
Use a tunnel stairwell — down from the surface, or up from the dark.
Murderous Intent
Kill a fellow passenger — a PlayerMob, or another player. Nobody saw anything.
Simon's Desperation
Give an item to a passenger and get nothing in return. Perhaps you'll get better luck next time.
Come On, Grab Your Friends
Trade gifts both ways with the same passenger. You give, they give. On this train, that's everything.
The Denamed
Give a passenger a name. Most of them have forgotten their own.
Reboarder
Help a passenger off the moving train. If they come back, they won't talk about it. Most are lying.
That's What Friends Are For
Kill a monster that was attacking a passenger. Dying together sucks a little less than dying alone.
Hello, Old Soul
Come within two blocks of your own echo — a passenger reincarnated from one of your past lives.
Old Friends, New Life
Befriend your own echo. Make peace with who you were.
Lay to Rest
Kill your own echo. Some pasts are better left behind.
Inter-Dimensional Passenger
Ride the train into the Nether, where the overworld gives way to netherrack and fire.
Can the train take you there?
Voided Warranty
Ride the train into the void, where the world has crumbled away to nothing beneath the rails.
Stay aboard past the point where the ground gives out.
End of the Line
Reach the floating End islands adrift in the disintegration band.
Ride on through the dark — pale islands drift out there.
Re-Over-World
Cross all the way through the void and the End, and ride back onto solid overworld ground.
All the way through the dark, and out the far side.
The Upside Down
Ride the train into the upside-down, where the sky mirrors the ground and the world hangs the wrong way round.
Some stretches of track don't just look wrong. They are wrong.
Reassembly Required
Ride out through the scattered islands as the upside-down disperses and the real overworld reassembles beneath you.
The world's stitching itself back together.
Repressed Memory
Shove your own echo off the train and into the void.
Meet your old self out over the abyss, and let go.
A Stranger's Ghost
Come within two blocks of someone else's echo — a passenger reincarnated from another player's past life.
Not every echo out here is yours.
Befriending the Dead
Befriend someone else's echo. Trade gifts both ways with a stranger's past life.
Be kind to a stranger you'll never meet.
Not Your Ghost to Keep
Kill someone else's echo.
Someone, somewhere, will feel that.
Erased
Shove someone else's echo off the train and into the void.
Off the train, into the nothing — they won't climb back.
Respect The Rules
Open 100 distinct chests in a single life. Open the same chest twice and the streak resets.
Taking Notes
Open ten random books. Re-reads count.
Don't know, Don't wana know
Burn 10 books without reading them
Nothing But Books
Fill your entire inventory with story books. Empty books don't count.
Ghostwriter
Sign a book and send it into the void — a stranger, somewhere, will read your words.
A Death Note
Sign a Death Note. When you fall, your echo will hunt the name you wrote.
Self-Fulfilling Prophecy
Write your own name in a Death Note.
Assassination Attempt
Kill a Death Note echo before it kills you.
A Stranger's Story
Open a chest book credited to a real passenger from another train. Someone out there wrote this for you.
Know The Rules
Read every Quiet Rule of the train. You know them all now — keeping them is another matter.
I Must Know
Read at least one variant of every Faulthurst volume.
Faulthurst's Favourite
Read every variant of every Faulthurst book. The margins are full now.
Collecting Stories
Read a single story from start to finish for the first time.
The Same But Different
Open ten starting books. They're not quite the same. They're not quite different.
Welcome Back
Read at least one variant of every starting book the train hands out.
Inter-Reality Passenger
Read every starting book — every variant, every context.
A Library In Motion
Read every letter of every narrative story carried by the train.
Every Reality, Every Word
Read every variant of every letter of every narrative story.
Dungeon Train Explorer
Traverse 100 carriages in a single life.
The Far Start
Carry your starting book beyond 150 carriages.
Pacifist
Traverse 100 carriages in a single life without dealing any damage.
Turn the Other Cheek
Traverse 250 carriages in a single life without dealing any damage.
Pure of Heart
Traverse 1,000 carriages in a single life without dealing any damage.
Far Afield
Ride through 10 different biomes in a single life.
Many Lands
Ride through 17 different biomes in a single life.
World Without End
Ride through 25 different biomes in a single life.
Terra Omnia
The whole world, and worlds beyond. Stand in every biome the Overworld, the Nether, and the End have to offer — all in a single life.
Long-Haul Passenger
Traverse 1,000 carriages in a single life.
So Many Potential Friends!
Encounter 1,000 players across all your runs.
Nearing Infinity
Traverse 10,000 carriages in a single life.
The Long Way Back
Traverse 100 carriages forward and 100 carriages back in a single life.
Over the Top
Run across the roof of a carriage group without dropping inside.
Skip the Grind
Run the length of 3 carriage groups along the train roof — in one go, without dropping inside.
Enjoying the Ride
Spend two hours on board the train. Time accumulates across every world.
Settling In
Spend ten hours aboard the train — long enough to settle in. Time accumulates across every world.
I Live Here Now
A full day on the rails — twenty-four hours aboard. The platform has long since gone. The horizon is just track. The train is home.
Help, I Can't Get Off
Three days on the rails. You've tried the doors. They don't open. They never did.
One Long Ride
Spend an unbroken hour aboard the train in a single life. The platform is a rumour now.
Reality Escaped
Spend two unbroken hours aboard the train in a single life — no death, no dismount. Just you and the rails.
Escaping Spawn
Ride 10,000 metres aboard the train in a single life.
Cross-Country
Ride 100,000 metres aboard the train in a single life.
The Long Run
Ride 250,000 metres aboard the train in a single life — without dying once.
Covering ground
Ride 100,000 metres aboard the train across all your runs.
I got so far
One million metres. You tried so hard and got so far — but in the end, does it even matter?
Nether Return Again
Loop all the way around — the Nether, the void, the End islands, back to solid ground — then ride the train into the Nether a second time.
Come Along With Me
Join a multiplayer world, or have someone join yours.
Others?
Am I alone?
Come within 4 blocks of another on the train.
The Enchiridion
Can you read?
So you have read a book. There are more…
Summon The Creator
He is in chat if you call
Tag @dev or @brennanhatton in chat
The Creator Answers
He doesn't always reply... but sometimes he does
Receive a message from the Developer
The Great Beyond
Complete the feedback survey after death.
advancements.dungeontrain.editor.made_ca: Create a new carriage from scratch in the editor.
Save a new carriage part template.
advancements.dungeontrain.editor.made_co: Add a new entry to a contents pool.
advancements.dungeontrain.editor.used_bl: Apply a block-variant entry to a cell.
Use a block-variant lock id to keep cells in sync.
advancements.dungeontrain.editor.saved_c: Save a contents template to disk.
advancements.dungeontrain.editor.saved_p: Save your authored content as a package.
advancements.dungeontrain.editor.exporte: Export a package zip ready to share.
advancements.dungeontrain.editor.made_tr: Save a new track tile variant.
advancements.dungeontrain.editor.made_pi: Save a new pillar section variant.
advancements.dungeontrain.editor.made_st: Save a new stair (pillar adjunct) variant.
